Defense_Rationale: Collects router configs with weak Cisco Type 7 passwords
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Eliminate plaintext/weak credentials,  use strong hashing (Type 8/9),  HSM-backed credential storage

Defense_Rationale: Passively collects PCAPs from specific ISP customer networks
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Restrict which roles can initiate packet capture or read local storage, log all PCAP access, DLP/monitoring can then flag bulk or unusual capture activity

Defense_Rationale: Enumerates interfaces/VRFs/routing/ACLs via CLI/SNMP
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Restrict access to configuration data,  monitor for bulk configuration queries,  SNMP v3 with encryption

Defense_Rationale: Enumerates and alters SNMP configs for lateral movement
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: SNMP write access restrictions,  community string rotation,  monitor for cross-device SNMP modifications

Defense_Rationale: Enables SSH servers and opens external-facing ports
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Alert on SSH server enablement,  port opening requires approval,  monitor for unexpected service activation

Defense_Rationale: Obfuscates source IPs in system logs
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Immutable logging to remote syslog,  correlate logs across devices,  detect source IP inconsistencies

Defense_Rationale: Double-encodes WSMA paths to exploit device web UI
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Input validation,  web application firewall,  monitor for encoding anomalies in API requests

Defense_Rationale: Configures SPAN/ERSPAN sessions to mirror traffic
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Audit SPAN session creation,  dual approval for monitoring port configuration,  alert on new mirror sessions

Defense_Rationale: Uses tunnels (IPsec/GRE) for exfiltration
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Segmentation and egress controls restrict where GRE/IPsec tunnels can originate and terminate, egress analysis and DLP then flag anomalous or high-volume tunnel usage

Defense_Rationale: Executes Python scripts (siet.py) on compromised devices
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Script execution policies,  application whitelisting,  monitor for unauthorized Python interpreter usage

Technique_Name: Command and Scripting: Network Device CLI
Defense_Rationale: Uses built-in CLI to execute native commands
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: CLI access restrictions,  privileged EXEC mode protections,  command logging and alerting

Defense_Rationale: Exploits CVE-2023-20273 for root privileges
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Patch CVE-2023-20273,  principle of least privilege limits post-compromise escalation impact

Defense_Rationale: Deletes/clears logs to remove forensic evidence
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Immutable append-only logging to remote collectors,  tamper-evident log storage,  alert on log deletion attempts

Defense_Rationale: Uses Guest Shell destroy command to remove artifacts
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Guest Shell monitoring and access restrictions prevent unauthorized container destruction commands, file system integrity checks detect artifact removal attempts, immutable forensic logging preserves evidence even after Guest Shell destruction

Defense_Rationale: Opens SSH/SFTP/RDP/FTP/HTTP(S) for C2
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Limit who can enable or expose SSH/SFTP/RDP/FTP/HTTP(S) on infrastructure devices (RBAC and ACLs), use behavioural analytics to detect unusual or long-lived service usage

Defense_Rationale: Leverages CLI to gather system information
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: CLI command restrictions, monitor for reconnaissance commands, least-privilege access reduces exposed information

Defense_Rationale: Uses VPS for C2 proxy
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Egress filtering and segmentation policies restrict which external hosts and networks infrastructure devices can reach, access control limits who can modify those egress policies

Defense_Rationale: Builds multi-hop relay chains (e.g. STOWAWAY)
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Restrict which principals can install or run additional binaries/containers on network devices, integrity monitoring and analytics then detect unexpected relay agents or daemons

Defense_Rationale: Uses GRE/IPsec to carry C2 over non-application protocols
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Segmentation and strict control over where GRE/IPsec can terminate limit options for non-application-layer C2, DPI then helps detect anomalous or unauthorized tunnels

Defense_Rationale: Plants SSH keys in authorized_keys for re-entry
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: File-system and configuration integrity checks on network devices (including authorized_keys) detect unauthorized key additions, account and key management policies prevent ad-hoc key insertion

Defense_Rationale: Brute forces weak Type 5/Type 7 passwords offline
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Strong password hashing (Type 8/9), account lockout policies, eliminate weak credential storage

Defense_Rationale: Creates new local users on network devices for persistence
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Centralized account governance and auditing (alerts on new or privileged local accounts) prevent stealthy persistence, integrity monitoring on device account databases detects tampering with account records

Defense_Rationale: Exploits CVE-2018-0171 and other known vulnerabilities
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Patching CVE-2018-0171 (7 years post-disclosure) eliminates exploitation vector, input validation reduces attack surface

Defense_Rationale: Abuses provider-to-provider and provider-customer trust
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Zero-trust architecture with mutual authentication, segment trusted interconnects with monitoring

Defense_Rationale: Leverages Guest Shell containers for persistence
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Container state monitoring, secure boot prevents unauthorized container deployment, attestation detects modifications

Defense_Rationale: Redirects TACACS+ to attacker server or weakens AAA
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Configuration governance prevents auth changes, HSM-backed authentication with attestation detects tampering,

Defense_Rationale: Compiles configurations and PCAPs on compromised device
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Segmentation and egress controls limit where infrastructure devices can send archive files, egress monitoring and DLP then flag large or unusual transfers from routers

Defense_Rationale: Modifies ACLs to permit attacker-controlled IPs
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Signed configuration with dual approval, real-time alerting on ACL changes, immutable audit trail

Defense_Rationale: Executes commands via SNMP on compromised devices
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Restrict SNMP write/exec privileges to tightly controlled management systems and accounts, SNMPv3 with strong authentication prevents credential theft via passive interception, monitoring flags unusual service-control commands

Defense_Rationale: Uses non-standard ports to evade detection
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Segmentation and egress policies strictly define which ports/services infrastructure devices may use, anomaly detection flags connections on unexpected ports

Defense_Rationale: Creates GRE/mGRE/IPsec tunnels for C2
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Tunnel creation requires config approval, alert on new tunnel establishment, audit tunnel configurations

Defense_Rationale: Pre-attack infrastructure setup outside defender control
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Threat intelligence identifies known malicious VPS infrastructure, egress filtering blocks connections to suspicious Ips

Defense_Rationale: Device compromise via software vulnerabilities
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Timely patching eliminates known CVEs, secure boot prevents unauthorized firmware

Defense_Rationale: Pre-attack preparation phase outside network
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Intelligence on attacker toolkits, cannot be prevented but can inform detection strategies

Defense_Rationale: Tool acquisition happens outside target network
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Threat intelligence on known exploit frameworks, vulnerability disclosure programs reduce exploit availability

Defense_Rationale: Topology gathering from compromised device config files
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Restrict access to configuration files, least-privilege reduces exposure of network topology information

Defense_Rationale: Scanning requires network access and reconnaissance capability
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Firewall rules and network access controls limit scanning surface, rate limiting detects reconnaissance

Defense_Rationale: Abuses peering connections to move between networks
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Peering connection auditing, monitor BGP route changes, bilateral telemetry at interconnects

Defense_Rationale: Targets MIB to collect network info via SNMP
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Restrict SNMP access to management networks and least-privilege accounts, SNMPv3 with authentication and encryption prevents credential theft via passive interception

Technique_Name: Data from Config Repository: Network Device
Defense_Rationale: Acquires credentials by collecting device configurations
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Encrypt or strongly hash stored secrets in configuration files so that exfiltrated configs do not yield reusable credentials, configuration access is logged and alerted on

Defense_Rationale: Uses Guest Shell container for reconnaissance and tool deployment
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Disable Guest Shell on production routers, monitor container state changes, restrict container capabilities

Defense_Rationale: Deploys Guest Shell to persist and evade monitoring
Why_Primary_Defense_Works: Container deployment restrictions, runtime integrity verification, monitor for unexpected container activation
